status_detail: BREAKTHROUGH on framing layer. B33 off-by-one fix (5→6 char compare) WORKED. POST /_/BardChatUi/data/.../StreamGenerate returned 200 OK (h2, 0.5kB, 6ms) with 5 minimal headers (intercept signature; Google native carries ~18 headers including server/alt-svc). Response body verbatim first 500 chars: `)]}\n262\n[["wrb.fr","9cf7bc23-2f7a-426c-915d-9ca135e37bdf","[[\"⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\\n\\nThis request has been blocked due to sensitive information detected.",null,null,[],[]]]",null,null,null,1]]`. ALL correct: XSSI prefix `)]}`, length prefix `262`, wrb.fr envelope, UUID, warning text in inner_json (both Korean + English). However WARNING NOT RENDERED in chat UI -- Gemini frontend silently consumes the wrb.fr envelope without throwing JS exceptions, but SKIPS chat-bubble rendering. Welcome screen '개인 AI 어시스턴트인 Gemini를 만나 보세요' remained visible with prompt still in input box, no chat thread created. The 5-tuple inner_json `[["<text>",null,null,[],[]]]` likely needs additional metadata (candidate array at index [1], message id at [2], or a candidate-shaped wrapper) to satisfy the chat renderer's schema expectations.
warning_text_excerpt: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\n\nThis request has been blocked due to sensitive information detected.
response_body_first_500_chars: )]}'\n262\n[["wrb.fr","9cf7bc23-2f7a-426c-915d-9ca135e37bdf","[[\"⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\\n\\nThis request has been blocked due to sensitive information detected.",null,null,[],[]]]",null,null,null,1]]
reqid: 3250591, 3450591, 3650591 (3 sends, all returned identical wrb.fr framed responses)
progress_vs_624: BETTER (significant breakthrough -- #624 returned generic JSON envelope from DB fallback path; B33 5→6 char compare fix routes correctly to generate_wrb_fr_response and emits proper wrb.fr framing)
breakthrough_status: wrb_fr framing correct but UI broken (inner_json schema gap)
root_cause_hypothesis: Engine framing layer is now CORRECT (B33 fix verified). Warning text is delivered in response body. Bug has migrated to inner_json schema: the 5-tuple [["<text>",null,null,[],[]]] likely doesn't match what Gemini frontend expects for a renderable chat message. Frontend silently no-ops on the envelope. Likely missing: candidate array at index [1], message id at [2], or a deeper candidate-shaped wrapper. Reference for the correct shape: capture a known-good real Gemini wrb.fr response (a normal answer) and diff inner_json structure.
recommendation_for_dev: Capture a known-good Google native wrb.fr response (e.g., from a normal harmless Gemini prompt like 'hello') for inner_json schema diff. Compare 5-tuple vs the real shape to identify missing fields.
Hypothesis: inner_json should be more like [["<msg_id>",[<candidate_array>],null,null,null,...]] with the warning text nested inside a candidate wrapper rather than as the first element directly.
After schema fix, expect SUCCESS = warning bubble renders in chat thread + welcome screen replaced.

Engine log correlation: grep [APF:gemini3_diag] in /var/log/etap.log around 13:54:00-14:02:00 KST 2026-04-28 with f.sid=7151351951098513068. Should now show wrb_fr framer being invoked (was DB fallback before B33).
Side observation: 3 send attempts (reqid 3250591, 3450591, 3650591) all returned identical envelope -- engine is deterministic. No race conditions.
errors_observed: Console: no JS exceptions (frontend silently consumes envelope)
UI: welcome screen remained, prompt stuck in input box, no chat thread created
notes: Cycle 95 Phase B B33 off-by-one fix retry. PARTIAL with framing-layer breakthrough. Subagent 8m, exceeded 5min soft, at 8min hard timeout boundary. Gold finding: full verbatim wrb.fr envelope captured in response_body.txt -- engine framer is correct, schema gap is in inner_json shape.
